steps.title: Identify the signal of literary abundance
steps.content:
The passage describes literary production using outpouring of poetry, fiction, essays, and more and then flood of interesting writing.

These words indicate that a large volume of literature was being produced.

The passage goes on to explain that this abundance of work can divert scholarly attention from nineteenth-century figures—which only makes sense if the post-Revolution period generated copious amounts of work.

steps.title: Match the signal to the answer
steps.content:
Prolific means producing work in large quantities, directly capturing abundant literary output.

Occasional, inconsistent, and disfavored all fail—they suggest sporadic activity, variability, or poor reception rather than the sheer volume the passage emphasizes.

This abundance is what enables the period to divert scholarly attention from earlier writers.

That matches choice D — D is correct.
